generated_oml:
instance InsulatedStyrofoamBox : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Insulated styrofoam box providing thermal insulation to minimize heat loss in the incubator system"
]

instance HeatBed : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Primary actuator for heating the incubator with unspecified actuation range, safety enforced via low-level driver"
]

instance Fan : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Actuator distributing heated air within the styrofoam box, controlled via controller commands"
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Internal temperature sensor monitoring temperature inside the styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempData1
]

instance InternalTemperatureSensor2 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Second internal temperature sensor monitoring temperature inside the styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:producedData InternalTempData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "External temperature sensor monitoring ambient temperature outside the styrofoam box"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ExternalTempData
]

instance InternalTempData1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor1
]

instance InternalTempData2 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom InternalTemperatureSensor2
]

instance ExternalTempData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance InternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature data from internal sensors (numerical values in °C)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTempData1, InternalTempData2
]

instance ExternalTemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Temperature data from external sensor (numerical values in °C)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ExternalTempData
]

instance RabbitMQMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open-source message broker (AMQP protocol) for asynchronous, reliable communication between components via RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureDataStreamService, ActuationCommandService
]

instance LowLevelDriver : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-level driver abstracting communication with sensors/actuators with safety protocols ensuring device stability"
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataUploadService
]

instance ModelAThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal model with two free parameters for temperature prediction (general lumped-parameter model)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEnabler
]

instance ModelBThermal : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Thermal model with four free parameters optimized for heatbed thermal inertia prediction (better dynamic behavior capture)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CoSimulationEnabler
]

instance KalmanFilter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Kalman filter for state estimation correlating measured temperatures with non-measured heatbed temperature for anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService, HeatbedStateEstimationService
]

instance TemperatureMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization dashboard with time-series data visualization of temperature trends"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureTrendInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Anomaly detection using Kalman filter to detect deviations from expected behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides ThermalAnomalyInsight
]

instance ControllerOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "What-if simulations to optimize controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) using co-simulation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance SelfAdaptationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop system for dynamic reconfiguration: anomaly detection, experiment scheduling, model recalibration"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveReconfigurationAction
]

instance TemperatureDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated temperature data stream from all sensors via RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:fromData InternalTemperatureData, ExternalTemperatureData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler
]

instance TimeSeriesDatabaseEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database (e.g., InfluxDB) for storing and visualizing temperature data trends"
    DTDFVocab:enables TemperatureMonitoringService
]

instance CoSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Co-simulation framework for what-if scenarios using historical/real-time data"
    DTDFVocab:enables ControllerOptimizationService
]

instance CalibrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Automated tracking simulator for Model B calibration using real-time sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelBThermal
]

instance BangBangController : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Bang-bang controller implementing temperature regulation with configurable parameters: LL (lower limit), UL (upper limit), H (heating duration), C (waiting duration)"
    DTDFVocab:contains HeatbedActuation, FanActuation
]

instance HeatbedActuation : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Actuation command for heatbed based on controller decisions (on/off via RabbitMQ)"
]

instance FanActuation : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Actuation command for fan distribution based on controller decisions (on/off via RabbitMQ)"
]

instance RabbitMQCommunicationServer : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central RabbitMQ server facilitating real-time message passing between Physical Twin and Digital Twin components"
    DTDFVocab:enables SensorDataUploadService, ActuationCommandService
]

instance ActuationCommandService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service distributing controller commands (fan/heatbed) via RabbitMQ to low-level driver"
    DTDFVocab:provides FanControlAction, HeatbedControlAction
]

instance SensorDataUploadService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service periodically reading temperature data from sensors and uploading to RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataUploadAction
]

instance TemperatureDataUploadAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for uploading temperature data from sensors to RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FanControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for controlling fan operation via RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HeatbedControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for controlling heatbed operation via RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControllerParameterAdjustment : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusting controller parameters (LL, UL, H, C) based on optimization simulations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ModelRecalibration : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recalibrating Model B parameters using new sensor data after anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TemperatureAdjustmentOverride : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual temperature adjustment override via dashboard interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance TemperatureTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time temperature trend analysis showing current and historical temperature patterns"
]

instance ThermalAnomalyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detection of temperature anomalies indicating potential system deviations or new content changes"
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Optimized controller parameter values (LL, UL, H, C) based on simulation results"
]

instance ParameterOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized controller parameter values (LL, UL, H, C) based on simulation results"
]

instance TemperatureDataStreamService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating temperature data from all sensors via RabbitMQ"
    DTDFVocab:provides TemperatureDataStream
]

instance AdaptiveReconfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action for dynamic reconfiguration: anomaly detection, experiment scheduling, model recalibration"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HeatbedStateEstimationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service estimating heatbed temperature state using Kalman filter"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance IncubatorEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains InsulatedStyrofoamBox, HeatBed, Fan, InternalTemperatureSensor1, InternalTemperatureSensor2, ExternalTemperatureSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RabbitMQCommunicationServer, LowLevelDriver, BangBangController, ModelAThermal, ModelBThermal, KalmanFilter, TemperatureMonitoringService, AnomalyDetectionService, ControllerOptimizationService, SelfAdaptationService
]


instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    **Command Types**:
    - Binary control commands: ON/OFF for the heatbed and fan (bang-bang control strategy, Document 6).
    - Temperature regulation commands (LL, UL, H, C parameters) from the controller to the low-level driver.

    **Control Message Formats**:
    - RabbitMQ messages (AMQP) for command transmission (Document 6).
    - Statechart-based control logic (Document 6) with parameters:
      - **LL**: Lower temperature limit (not specified numerically).
      - **UL**: Upper temperature limit (not specified numerically).
      - **H**: Heating duration (not specified numerically).
      - **C**: Waiting duration (not specified numerically).

    **Validation & Authentication**:
    - No explicit authentication; RabbitMQ ensures message reliability via acknowledgments and retries.

    **Feedback Systems**:
    - Real-time temperature feedback from sensors to the controller (Document 5).
    - Kalman filter (Document 5) estimates heatbed state from sensor data.

    **Conflict Resolution**:
    - Implicitly handled via the controller’s statechart (Document 8) and safety protocols in the low-level driver (Document 6).
    - Calibration loop (Document 5) resolves discrepancies between model predictions and sensor data.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
    - **Temperature sensor reading frequency**: Periodic (not explicitly quantified, but implied to be **low-level driver** polling at a rate consistent with thermal dynamics of the incubator, likely **seconds to minutes** range).
    - **Controller actuation cycle**:
      - **Heating duration (H)**: Not specified numerically, but inferred from thermal inertia modeling (Model B accounts for heatbed inertia, suggesting **longer heating cycles** than simple step responses).
      - **Waiting duration (C)**: Not quantified, but critical for thermal stabilization (likely **seconds to tens of seconds** to avoid overshoot).
    - **Data synchronization**: RabbitMQ message broker ensures **near-real-time** (<1 second) communication between **Physical Twin (PT)** and **Digital Twin (DT)** components (RabbitMQ is designed for low-latency messaging).
    - **Model update frequency**: Implied during **on-demand calibration** (explicitly mentioned in Document 5) for Model B, triggered by anomalies detected via Kalman filter (no explicit timeframe, but likely **minutes to hours** for full recalibration).
    - **Simulation granularity**: Co-simulations use **historical/real-time data** with **time-series granularity** (e.g., per-second resolution for temperature trends).
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
    - **Core architecture pattern**:
      - **CPS-based Digital Twin framework** (as depicted in Fig. 1: Cyber ↔ Physical integration via models, algorithms, and data).
      - **Event-driven orchestration**: Components (low-level driver, controller) publish/subscribe via RabbitMQ for real-time updates.
    - **Component integration**:
      - **Data flow**:
        - **PT → DT**: Temperature data from sensors → RabbitMQ → DT models/algorithms.
        - **DT → PT**: Control commands (fan/heatbed actuation) → RabbitMQ → low-level driver.
      - **Model-Driven Simulation**: Models (Model A/B) are calibrated dynamically via **on-demand calibration** (Document 5).
      - **Algorithmic loops**:
        - **Kalman filter** (state estimation for heatbed temperature).
        - **Self-adaptation loop** (8-step workflow for dynamic controller reconfiguration).
    - **Resource management**:
      - **RabbitMQ** handles message queuing with **low-latency** requirements (critical for thermal control stability).
      - **No explicit cloud/edge deployment** mentioned; all components co-located on Raspberry Pi and local infrastructure.
    - **System-wide coordination**:
      - **Centralized RabbitMQ broker** ensures **unified message exchange** between PT and DT.
      - **No federated DTs** or cross-system coordination described (focused on single-incubator DT).
  "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    **Development and Deployment Phases:**
    - **Modeling Phase:**
      - Creation of abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for static properties, dynamic models via differential equations for dynamics).
      - Two thermal models for the incubator are developed:
        - **Model A**: Simplified with 2 free parameters (e.g., for basic temperature approximation).
        - **Model B**: Enhanced with 4 free parameters (better for capturing heatbed inertia).
      - Calibration methods (e.g., automated tracking simulator) refine model parameters against real-time sensor data.

    - **Digital Twin Integration Phase:**
      - Models are integrated into a **Digital Twin (DT)** framework, combining:
        - **Data**: Real-time sensor data (e.g., 3 temperature sensors: 2 inside the styrofoam box, 1 outside).
        - **Models**: Cyber-physical models (e.g., Model B for heatbed inertia) and environmental models.
        - **Algorithms**: Fault detection (Kalman filter), state estimation, and optimization (e.g., controller parameter tuning via co-simulations).
      - **Lifecycle Governance**:
        - Continuous calibration (on-demand) via automated experiments (e.g., cooling/heating cycles) to adapt to changes (e.g., new objects like cold water buckets).
        - Version control implied by open-source availability (GitHub repository for hardware/software specs).

    - **Operational Phase:**
      - Real-time visualization (dashboard with time-series data from RabbitMQ).
      - Self-adaptation loop (closed-loop DT):
        1. Anomaly detection (Kalman filter triggers).
        2. Data collection (experiment design).
        3. Model recalibration (Model B).
        4. Controller reconfiguration.
        5. What-if simulations for optimization.
      - Transition between phases is managed via **automated calibration/validation pipelines** (e.g., co-simulation workflows).

    **Representation Types:**
    - Static: CAD-inspired (e.g., styrofoam box geometry).
    - Dynamic: Differential equation-based (e.g., Model B).
    - Runtime: Real-time DT with RabbitMQ-based data streams.
  "
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
    **Model Fidelity Requirements:**
    - **Model A vs. Model B**:
      - **Model A**: Sufficient for basic temperature prediction but lacks thermal inertia detail.
      - **Model B**: Higher fidelity due to **four free parameters**, enabling accurate thermal inertia modeling (e.g., heatbed’s mass/thermal properties).
    - **Abstraction Levels**:
      - **High-Level**: Geometric/behavioral models (e.g., styrofoam box insulation, fan distribution).
      - **Low-Level**: Dynamic models (e.g., differential equations for thermal response).
    - **Trade-offs**: Fidelity vs. computational cost (e.g., Model B’s complexity slows simulation).

    **Validation and Verification Methods:**
    - **Calibration**: Experimental comparison between Model A/B and real-world data (e.g., temperature response curves).
      - **Method**: Parameter optimization via iterative testing (e.g., Model B’s calibration in Document 5).
    - **Uncertainty Quantification**:
      - **Thermal Inertia**: Model B’s parameters are tuned to minimize prediction errors (e.g., via least-squares fitting).
      - **Safety Margins**: Implicit in the controller’s LL/UL thresholds (e.g., ±5°C buffer).
    - **Error Detection/Correction**:
      - **Anomaly Detection**: Kalman filter detects deviations from expected behavior (e.g., new objects altering thermal dynamics).
      - **Self-Adaptation Loop**: Automated recalibration (e.g., Steps 5–7 in Document 5) corrects model inaccuracies.

    **Quality Assurance Processes:**
    - **Hardware-Software Integration**: Open-source repository ensures reproducibility (e.g., GitHub repository [link]).
    - **Experimental Validation**: Model B’s superiority validated via **thermal inertia experiments** (Document 5).
    - **Real-Time Validation**: Continuous monitoring via RabbitMQ ensures data integrity for dashboards/optimizations.
  "
]

    - No mention of bandwidth constraints, but RabbitMQ’s scalability supports high-frequency data streams.

    **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:

]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
    **Hosting Platforms:**
    - **Primary deployment**:
      - **Raspberry Pi** (Document 3, 4) as the primary host for the **controller** and **low-level driver**.
      - **RabbitMQ server** deployed on-premises (Document 6).
      - **Optional cloud integration**: RabbitMQ supports cloud deployment (Document 6).

    **Computational & Storage Requirements:**
    - **Raspberry Pi**:
      - Lightweight computational tasks (control logic, statechart execution).
      - No explicit storage requirements mentioned, but data is stored in **time-series databases** (implied for visualization, Document 5).
    - **RabbitMQ**:
      - Handles real-time message brokering (Document 6).
      - Storage for historical data (e.g., temperature logs).

    **Scalability Mechanisms:**
    - **RabbitMQ**:
      - Supports horizontal scaling (Document 6).
      - Handles tens of thousands of users (Document 6).
    - **No explicit scalability for Raspberry Pi** mentioned, but control loop is lightweight.

    **Deployment Models Supported:**
    - **On-premises** (Document 6).
    - **Cloud** (Document 6, via RabbitMQ).
    - **Edge computing** (implied for Raspberry Pi deployment, Document 4).

    **Availability & Reliability Assurance:**
    - **RabbitMQ**:
      - High availability via clustering (Document 6).
      - Persistent message queues.
    - **Raspberry Pi**:
      - No redundancy mentioned, but assumed to be single-point deployment.
      - **No explicit uptime guarantees** mentioned.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
    **Hardware-Specific Safety Features:**
    - **Temperature Monitoring:**
      - Two internal temperature sensors for precise in-box temperature measurement.
      - One external temperature sensor for environmental monitoring.
      - **Calibration Algorithms:** Used to update cyber-physical system (CPS) models with real-time sensor data for accurate parameter estimation (Document 3, Fig. 1).

    **Systemic Safety Mechanisms:**
    - **Insulated Container:** Styrofoam box for thermal insulation to prevent uncontrolled heat loss, ensuring stable internal temperature regulation.
    - **Heatbed and Fan Integration:** Physical plant components (heatbed + fan) dynamically distribute heat within the insulated box, enabling controlled thermal management.
    - **Cyber-Physical Integration:**
      - Raspberry Pi-based controller for real-time monitoring and actuation (Document 4, Fig. 2).
      - **Model-Based Calibration:** Dynamic models (e.g., differential equations) are employed to predict thermal behavior, enabling predictive safety checks (Document 3, [3, Section 5]).
      - **Fault Detection/Supervisory Control:** Implicitly referenced in supervisory control frameworks (Document 13) for detecting deviations from expected behavior.

    **Technologies/Methodologies:**
    - **Formal Verification:** Mentioned in FMI-enabled data broker (RabbitMQ FMU) for ensuring correctness of simulation-to-reality mappings (Document 17).
    - **Self-Adaptive Systems:** Rapid prototyping via Python Functional Mockup Units (FMUs) for adaptive control (Document 15).
    - **Autonomic Computing Principles:** Algorithms for self-optimization and fault tolerance (Document 9, [9]) are implied in DT workflows (Document 3).

    **Contextual Notes:**
    - No explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, authentication) or access control mechanisms, but the DT framework (Document 3) integrates models@runtime (Document 11, 12) for dynamic adaptation, which inherently includes safety-critical validation.
    - **Safety-Critical System Considerations:** The incubator’s design prioritizes thermal robustness over cybersecurity, but the DT’s calibration and monitoring loops (Document 3) suggest a layered safety approach (physical + cyber).
  "
]
